content.verdict: Rohotu Fare Lodge is the single most distinctive honeymoon choice on Bora Bora — four hilltop traditional Polynesian fares (bungalows) built by the Nir family using local timber, thatched pandanus, volcanic stone and genuine craftsmanship. There is no resort here: no pool, no restaurant, no reception desk, no front-of-house — just four fares spread across a lush hillside garden with panoramic views straight down onto the lagoon and Mount Otemanu. Your host Nir personally welcomes you, drives you around, arranges lagoon tours with the best local operators, and treats you like family. Each fare has a kitchenette (he stocks it before you arrive if you ask), an outdoor shower under the stars, hand-carved wood detailing, and a private deck with one of the finest views in all of French Polynesia. The beach is not on-site (you drive or he drops you at Matira 10 minutes away), and this is the main tradeoff honeymooners evaluate. For couples who want Bora Bora stripped of corporate polish — actually Polynesian, actually local, actually intimate — this is the pick that makes other resorts feel sterile afterward. TripAdvisor #1 ranked on the island for years for good reason.
content.best_room: All four fares have spectacular views; the newer ones feature slightly larger decks and more modern bathrooms. Ask Nir directly which fare he recommends for your dates — availability and personality vary, and he matches guests to fares thoughtfully. Honeymoon tip: request the highest-elevation fare for the most dramatic sunset over Otemanu.
caveats: No beach on-site — you drive or are driven to Matira (10 min).
No restaurant, no pool, no spa, no room service.
This is a self-catering situation with kitchenette; eating out requires planning.
Rental car or scooter strongly recommended.
Only 4 fares total, so book 6-12 months ahead for honeymoon dates.
No overwater bungalow — if that is the dealbreaker, pair Rohotu (3 nights) with a motu resort (4 nights).
Steep path up to some fares; not mobility-friendly.

faqs.a: It is a guesthouse / lodge — four independently built traditional fares run by the Nir family. Closer to a premium B&B or private villa rental than a resort, but with a level of personal hospitality no resort can match.

faqs.q: Why is it ranked #1 on TripAdvisor?
faqs.a: Because of the hosts. Nir and his family personalize the experience, the views are among the best on the island, and the price-to-experience ratio is extraordinary. Couples consistently describe it as the highlight of their French Polynesia trip.

faqs.q: No overwater bungalow — is that a mistake for a honeymoon?
faqs.a: Only if overwater was your only reason for coming. Many honeymooners pair Rohotu (authentic, local, panoramic) with a short 2-3 night overwater stay for the cliché shot. The combination is frequently called the best of both worlds.

faqs.q: Do we need a rental car?
faqs.a: Strongly recommended. Bora Bora's main-island road is 32 km and you will want mobility for restaurants, beaches and shops. Nir can help arrange a rental car or scooter.
Yes — full fridge, stove, coffee setup, dishware. Nir can pre-stock breakfast essentials and cooking ingredients if you request in advance.

faqs.q: How far in advance to book?
faqs.a: 6-12 months, especially for May-October. With only 4 fares, it sells out fast for honeymoon season.

Hi Nir,

We are planning our honeymoon on Bora Bora and would love to stay at Rohotu Fare for 7 nights, arriving [date].

- Availability for our dates and which fare you would recommend for a honeymoon
- Airport pickup and rental car arrangements
- Breakfast pre-stocking option
- Recommendations for a lagoon tour, La Villa Mahana reservation, and a private sunset cruise
- Any welcome touches you suggest for honeymooners

We have heard extraordinary things about your place and cannot wait.

Thank you,
[Names]
